title: Physical and Logical Security, Identity, Authentication, and Access · Causal Recall
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs physical and logical security, identity, authentication, and access from user impact, asset identity, safety, baseline, symptom, recent change, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, verification, prevention, escalation, documentation, and transfer evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for identity and least-privilege access model after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, ca…
objectives: Classify physical, technical, administrative, preventive, deterrent, detective, corrective, compensating, and directive controls by threat and evidence.
Separate person, device, workload, account, identifier, credential, authenticator, authentication, authorization, session, role, group, and audit event.
Compare knowledge, possession, inherence, location, behavior, certificates, tokens, biometrics, MFA, passwordless, fallback, recovery, and enrollment risks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Security Control Types
steps.instruction:
Order the Security Control Types CompTIA A+ support HTTP evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Security Control Types.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Security Control Types assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For CompTIA A+ support Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Security Control Types, keep Security Control Types connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: People, accounts, credentials, devices, roles, badges, sessions, groups, guests, and service identities are conflated after unauthorized access. The review variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Classify physical, technical, administrative, preventive, deterrent, detective, corrective, compensating, and directive controls by threat and evidence.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Security Control Types has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

options.text: Declare CompTIA A+ support user outcome, fictional ticket, asset and environment identity, authorization, hazards, accessibility and privacy needs, baseline, and stop conditions for the review Security Control Types case

stimulus.title: Physical Access case record

lines.label: assumption

lines.label: risk

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Layer doors, locks, badges, guards, cameras, lighting, visitor controls, equipment locks, privacy screens, secure areas, tailgating resistance, and emergency access. Artifact under review: identity and least-privilege access model.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Layer doors, locks, badges, guards, cameras, lighting, visitor controls, equipment locks, privacy screens, secure areas, tailgating resistance, and emergency access.
